doc.title: Restricted stock — grant, vest, forfeit
doc.event: On January 1, Drew Corp. grants an executive 4,000 restricted shares ($1 par) when the stock trades at $25, vesting over 4 years. The executive leaves after year 2. Prepare the grant, annual, and forfeiture entries.

entries.caption: Grant date — shares issued, compensation deferred

lines.label: 4,000 × $25 FV
lines.why: The full grant-date fair value parks in Unearned Compensation — a CONTRA-EQUITY account that drains into expense as service is rendered.
lines.trap: Contra-equity, not an asset — it's future services owed by the employee, shown as a reduction of equity until earned.

lines.label: 4,000 × $1 par
lines.why: The shares legally exist from grant day — par to the stock account now.
lines.trap: Restricted stock issues REAL shares at grant (options don't issue anything until exercise) — that's why this entry exists on day one.

lines.account: Paid-in Capital in Excess of Par — Common
lines.label: the excess
lines.why: FV over par, standard issuance mechanics.
lines.trap: Net equity effect at grant = ZERO (credits 100,000, contra-debit 100,000). Equity grows only as the expense runs.

lines.why: Straight-line over the service period, measured at grant-date FV — same photograph principle as options.
lines.trap: Later stock prices are irrelevant; $25 at grant is the number forever (unless forfeited).

lines.label: drains toward zero
lines.why: The contra account releases as service is earned — 100,000 → 75,000 → 50,000 → 25,000 → 0.
lines.trap: Credit Unearned Compensation here, not PIC — the equity was already credited in full at grant.

variants.label: Executive leaves after year 2 — unwind everything

entries.caption: Forfeiture: reverse the shares AND the expense taken

lines.label: shares cancelled
lines.why: The unvested shares are cancelled — par comes back off.
lines.trap: Full unwind: the grant entry runs in reverse…

lines.account: Paid-in Capital in Excess of Par — Common
lines.label: excess reversed
lines.trap: …AND the expense already booked comes back too:

lines.label: 2 years reversed
lines.why: Service condition failed, so the 2 × 25,000 already expensed REVERSES — the compensation was never actually earned.
lines.trap: This is the sharp contrast with option EXPIRATION (no reversal). Forfeiture = condition failed = unwind; expiration = earned but unused = keep the expense.

lines.label: remaining balance clears
lines.why: The undrained 50,000 of the contra account clears with the cancelled grant.
lines.trap: Check the unwind: all four lines together restore every account to its pre-grant balance.

computationPaths.narration: Forfeiture arithmetic: expense reversed = years served × annual (2 × 25,000 = 50,000); Unearned Compensation cleared = the undrained remainder (100,000 − 50,000).

memorize.body: Restricted stock: grant issues real shares vs Unearned Compensation (contra-equity) at grant-date FV; expense straight-line over service; forfeiture unwinds shares AND reverses expense taken.
Options expire → expense stays. Restricted stock forfeits → expense reverses. 'Did they finish earning it?' decides which rule fires.

questions.prompt: 4,000 restricted shares (100,000 grant-date FV, 4-year vesting) are forfeited when the executive quits after year 2. Compensation expense in the forfeiture entry is…

distractors.feedback: Zero-reversal is the option-EXPIRATION rule. Restricted-stock forfeiture means the service condition failed — the 2 years of expense (50,000) reverses as a CREDIT to Compensation Expense.
Only what was EXPENSED reverses through expense (2 × 25,000). The other 50,000 was still sitting in Unearned Compensation and clears from there.

accountBank.account: Paid-in Capital in Excess of Par — Common

build.scaffold: Grant = full issuance offset by contra-equity (net zero). Each year = drain 1/N into expense. Forfeit = run the film backward — shares, excess, the drained expense, and the undrained remainder, all home.
